description: R81 subset-schema pre-gate hardening (no-live, tooling). Closes the R80 pre-gate gap by validating REALITY/VLESS subset schema in the dry-run pre-gate stage.

tooling_change.summary: Closes the R80 pre-gate gap. The rust app's Config::load rejects unknown fields at /outbounds/i/... (e.g. GUI-only __id_in_gui), but the dry-run path in reality_vless_probe_batch.py never loaded the subset through the rust binary, so the rejection only surfaced at live matrix time. R81 adds a subset-schema gate that runs in the dry-run pre-gate stage.
rules: outbound-level fields with prefix `__` are rejected (catches GUI-only fields like `__id_in_gui` that R80 hit)
outbound-level fields not in the curated REALITY/VLESS allow-list are rejected (separate branch from the prefix rule)
nested fields with prefix `__` are rejected at any depth (covers e.g. tls.__leaked_meta, transport.__gui_path_hint)
nested whitelisting is NOT enforced; the rust loader's nested schema is large and protocol-shape-specific, matching it exactly is out of scope
field VALUES are never read or surfaced in violations; only path + field name + reason
tooling_change.allow_list_scope: REALITY/VLESS only; NOT a union of every protocol's outbound fields

compat_audit.reality_vless_confirmation_cohorts: no break — operates on round_summary shape; does not consume probe_batch dry-run output
compat_audit.reality_vless_probe_plan: no break — uses dict.get and pre-existing fields only (selected_count, counts, selected); new gate fields are forward-compatible additions
compat_audit.reality_vless_probe_evidence: no break — uses dict.get on plan/summary; new gate fields are forward-compatible additions

tooling_change.behavior_when_gate_fails: dry-run writes plan.json and summary.json with subset_schema_gate_passed=false and the violations list, prints stdout payload, then exits with status 2 so cohort/orchestration scripts treat the dry-run as not-passed
tooling_change.behavior_when_gate_passes: dry-run output gains subset_schema_gate_passed=true and exits 0 as before
tooling_change.behavior_on_live_run: live (non-dry-run) path is unchanged; subset_schema_gate_passed and subset_schema_gate keys are absent on the live shape so no downstream consumer is forced to handle them

branch_coverage: double-underscore prefix rule at outbound level
non-underscore unknown field at outbound level (whitelist branch, distinct reason)
redaction: violations carry only path/field/reason, no raw values
dry-run integration: gate failure -> exit 2, plan/summary/stdout carry subset_schema_gate_passed=false
dry-run integration: gate pass -> exit 0, plan/summary carry subset_schema_gate_passed=true
live path unchanged: no gate keys on plan/summary
allow-list scope: required fields in, foreign-protocol fields out

classification.label: actionable; tooling hardening; no live; no node contact
classification.rationale: R81 closes a structural pre-gate gap exposed by R80 (subset schema mismatch only fires at live matrix time). The fix is a no-live, no-node-contact, no-sampler/dataplane-touching tooling addition that surfaces the failure class at dry-run time, before any live authorization is granted. Future fresh-cohort live runs (fresh04 retest, cohort C round-2, R73 unselected recovery nodes) will benefit from this gate. BHV 52/56 unchanged; not a parity completion; not a sampler/dataplane regression.
classification.next_handling: R82 (proposed) would be the fresh04 same-failure recheck with a cleansed subset; requires explicit user re-authorization per the existing cohort-authorization discipline. R81 itself does not authorize any live run.

follow_up.next_round_recommendation: R82 fresh04 same-failure recheck with cleansed subset (same scope as R80: fresh04 only, REALITY/VLESS only, x3 runs, no auto-extension)

interpretation: R80 returned classification C (tooling/config blocker). The blocker was that the rust app config validator rejected `__id_in_gui` carried over from a neutralized REALITY subset; the pre-gate dry-run did not catch it because dry-run does not load the config in the rust app process.

R81 closes that gap by adding a subset-schema gate at the dry-run stage. The gate is REALITY/VLESS-scoped and enforces two distinct rules at the outbound level (prefix rejection and allow-list) plus a recursive prefix rule at any depth. Field values are never read or surfaced; violations carry only path/field/reason.
The gate runs only in dry-run mode; the live path is unchanged. Operators are expected to pass the dry-run gate before authorizing a live run, which matches the existing pre-gate discipline.

Compat audit: reality_vless_confirmation_cohorts.py operates on round_summary shape and does not consume probe_batch dry-run output; reality_vless_probe_plan.py and reality_vless_probe_evidence.py both use dict.get on existing fields. Adding subset_schema_gate_passed and subset_schema_gate to dry-run output is forward-compatible.

BHV remains 52/56. R81 is not dual-kernel parity completion. R81 is not sampler/dataplane regression and does not modify go_fork_source/* or .github/workflows/*. Live reality/vless probe authorization remains separate; fresh04 same-failure recheck with a cleansed subset is proposed as R82 and requires explicit user re-authorization.
